title: athyrium otophorum
intro: Athyrium otophorum, also known as the Omeo fern, is a type of fern native to Australia, specifically in the eastern and southern regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Athyrium otophorum include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce pain and inflammation. Herbal preparations of Athyrium otophorum can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and powdering the leaves for use in capsules or tinctures.
